mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a healthy and robust labor market, which supports strong consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, low unemployment correlates with economic expansion.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods where UNRATE is below 5% for at least 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong retail sales often precede positive earnings reports.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are flat or negative in more than 50% of periods where RSAFS 3-month annualized growth exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is low and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also contained, it signals an accommodative monetary environment with stable prices, which encourages investment and boosts equity valuations due to lower discount rates and higher real earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:Low rates and low inflation typically support higher P/E multiples.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns show no significant outperformance (Rank IC < 0.03) when both FEDFUNDS is below 3% and CPIAUCSL 12-month change is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes or accompanies economic contractions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns, especially during periods of economic stress.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Recessions are often marked by sharp increases in unemployment.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points in 3 months during a declared recessionary period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer and business confidence, indicating future economic activity and demand for goods and services, which supports positive equity market performance over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing activity is a leading indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If the HOUST month-over-month growth rate has no statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 5-day returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, signaling weakening economic conditions that negatively impact corporate earnings and equity prices over the short to medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production declines are associated with economic downturns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of periods when INDPRO 3-month annualized growth falls below -3%.

mechanism_narrative: High and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL), especially when accompanied by rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS), erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, often leading to tighter monetary policy and negative S&P 500 returns as discount rates increase and future earnings are devalued.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation often leads to central bank tightening.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month change is greater than 3% and FEDFUNDS has increased over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a healthy and growing economy driven by consumer strength, which provides a strong tailwind for corporate earnings and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Employment and retail sales are key indicators of consumer-driven growth.
failure_condition: If the combination of PAYEMS 3-month growth greater than 0.1% and RSAFS 3-month growth greater than 0.5% does not lead to statistically significantly positive S&P 500 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05).
